3A_Bibliographic.citation: Dall'Aglio, L., Labrecque, J. A., Schuurmans, I., Zhang, Y., Creasey, N., Wilson, M., Kennedy, C. J., Muetzel, R. L., Smoller, J. W., Tiemeier, H., & Choi, K. W. (2025). Evaluating hypothetical prevention strategies for internalizing symptoms in the general population and at-risk children. Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 93(4), 252-266.
3A_Bibliographic.journal: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology
3A_Bibliographic.notes: Also uses Generation R Study (N=3,739) but we code only the ABCD portion

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: We used data from ABCD release 4.0. The ABCD Study is based at 21 sites in the United States and recruited children aged 9-10 in 2015-2017... The final (universal prevention strategy) sample size for the ABCD and GenR studies comprised 8,699 and 3,739 children, respectively.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: Multiple sample sizes for different prevention settings: universal N=8,699; selective genetics N=1,207; selective life events N=2,505; indicated N=1,954

3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous hours per day; also examined as categorical guideline adherence (0, 1, 2, 3, 4 hr/day compared to existing guideline of 2 hr/day)
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: Daily hours of recreational ST were calculated based on parent reports in both cohorts. Sleep duration (hours per day) was reported by parents in ABCD (9-11 hr; 8-9 hr; <8 hr)... PA was assessed based on self-reports in terms of days per week of at least 1 hr of moderate-to-vigorous (MVPA) exercise in ABCD.
3C_Exposure.notes: Screen time is one of several modifiable factors examined (also PA, sleep, social support). Screen time modeled with splines due to nonlinear association.

3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: Brief Problem Monitor (BPM, 19 items) for ABCD
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: Internalizing symptoms (e.g., symptoms of sadness, fear, or worthlessness) were extracted from the Brief Problem Monitor (BPM) for ABCD (T1 and T2)... Raw scores were used. Internalizing problems were then scaled (M = 0, SD = 1) for interpretability.

3E_Design_Analysis.study_design: prospective cohort with target trial emulation and G-formula
3E_Design_Analysis.model_type: linear regression (TTE) + G-formula for causal estimation
3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Two-step approach: (1) Target trial emulation using longitudinal linear regression with splines for ST; (2) G-formula to estimate average treatment effects (ATEs) for hypothetical interventions. Bootstrapping (1,000 reps) for CIs.
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex, parent-reported race of child, pubertal status, parental education, household income, maternal age at childbirth, parental psychopathology, study site (with residualization/adjustments), baseline internalizing symptoms
3E_Design_Analysis.SES_in_model: yes - household income and parental education included as covariates in all models
3E_Design_Analysis.multiple_comparisons_correction: Hierarchical approach for testing across prevention settings
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: In the first analytical part, we used a causal observational framework, TTE (Hernan & Robins, 2020) to improve our observational study design by mimicking hypothetical RCT conditions... In the second analytical part, G-formula was used to estimate unobserved outcomes (change in internalizing symptoms) that would have occurred for unobserved exposures (change in modifiable factors)... All models were controlled for age, sex, parent-reported race of the child (ABCD)... pubertal status, parental education, household income, maternal age at childbirth, parental psychopathology, and study site.
3E_Design_Analysis.notes: This is a causal inference paper, not a standard regression. TTE framework excludes children with clinically relevant internalizing at baseline and adjusts for baseline internalizing.

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time (Spline 1) - TTE framework, ABCD universal
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Change in internalizing symptoms in SD units
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: In the universal setting, under the TTE conditions, greater ST was associated with more internalizing symptoms over time in ABCD, ABCD_screen-universal (Spline 1): estimate = 0.275 SDs; SE = 0.088; p value = .002

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time - G-formula ATE: 0 hr vs 2 hr/day (stricter guideline), ABCD universal
3F_Effect_Sizes.effect_type: ATE (average treatment effect in SD units)
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Negative = reduction in internalizing symptoms under stricter guideline
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: following more stringent (intervention arm A: 0 or 1 hr/day) rather than existing guidelines (intervention arm B: 2 hr/day) for ST was estimated to reduce internalizing symptoms by 0.09-0.16 SDs (95% CI_ABCD [-0.14, -0.04]; 95% CI_GenR [-0.25, -0.06]) for 0 hr/day

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time - G-formula ATE: 1 hr vs 2 hr/day (stricter guideline), ABCD universal
Screen time - G-formula ATE: 3 hr vs 2 hr/day (lenient guideline), ABCD universal
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Positive = increase in internalizing symptoms under lenient guideline
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Table 3: 3 hr vs 2 hr, ABCD ATE = 0.037, 95% CI [0.017, 0.056]

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time - G-formula ATE: 4 hr vs 2 hr/day (lenient guideline), ABCD universal
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Positive = increase in internalizing symptoms under lenient guideline
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Table 3: 4 hr vs 2 hr, ABCD ATE = 0.065, 95% CI [0.030, 0.099]

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time (Spline 1) - TTE, ABCD indicated prevention
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Negative direction (higher ST, lower internalizing in spline 2 for indicated setting in ABCD)
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: ABCD_ST-indicated: estimate = -0.625 SDs; SE = 0.246; p value = .011, not ABCD for the first spline, while lower internalizing symptoms were observed in ABCD in the second spline

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time - TTE, GenR indicated prevention (Spline 1)
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: GenR_ST-indicated: estimate = 0.991 SDs; SE = 0.389; p value = .011

3G_Reporting_Quality.abstract_uses_significance_language: No - abstract does not use 'significant' framing
3G_Reporting_Quality.small_es_verbatim: Although small effects have led to reservations about centering public health initiatives on ST for the prevention and management of youth depression risk (Orben & Przybylski, 2019), our findings suggest that encouraging healthy ST habits may still be meaningful... interventions could reduce internalizing symptoms by approximately 0.10 SD, to further limit depression risk
3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: Paper acknowledges small effects explicitly (cites Orben & Przybylski) but then argues they may still be meaningful at population level. This is an acknowledge-then-override pattern. Effect sizes are very small: max ATE of 0.09-0.16 SDs in universal setting.

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: Overall, we provide quantitative estimates regarding potential guidelines for key modifiable factors that can be further tested in real-world trials, in the context of the primary prevention of depression in young people... results suggest that reducing ST, or at least limiting increases in ST, could be particularly beneficial for preventing internalizing problems in indicated intervention scenarios... subgroups of the population with subclinical baseline symptoms (indicated risk), may warrant particular attention and potentially benefit from tailored prevention recommendations (e.g., more stringent guidelines).

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.pattern_detail: Paper explicitly acknowledges small effect sizes and cites Orben & Przybylski (2019) skepticism, but then argues effects 'may still be meaningful' at population level. Uses causal inference framing (TTE, G-formula) to elevate the findings above standard observational associations. Frames ST as a modifiable lifestyle factor amenable to intervention. The maximum ATE in the universal setting is 0.09-0.16 SDs - very small effects - yet the paper recommends potential guideline changes and tailored prevention recommendations.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.population_override_verbatim: Although small effects have led to reservations about centering public health initiatives on ST for the prevention and management of youth depression risk... our findings suggest that encouraging healthy ST habits may still be meaningful and reduce internalizing symptoms from childhood into adolescence at an overall, population-based level.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.modifiable_factor_verbatim: This study quantifies the potential impact of relaxing versus adopting more stringent screen time and physical activity guidelines on internalizing symptoms
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The paper leads with the causal inference framework rather than significance. It acknowledges that universal effects are small but emphasizes that indicated (at-risk) effects are larger. It explicitly cites the small-effect critique from the literature and then overrides it by arguing population-level relevance. The overall framing is careful and nuanced relative to many papers in this literature, but it still issues prevention recommendations based on ATEs of 0.04-0.16 SDs.

3I_Flags.ambiguity_notes: Screen time is one of several modifiable factors examined (PA, sleep, social support). Only ST showed significant longitudinal association under TTE. Paper uses both ABCD and GenR cohorts but they are not meta-analyzed due to measurement heterogeneity. The causal inference design (TTE + G-formula) is more sophisticated than standard regression but still relies on observational data with assumptions about confounding.
